title: Analyze flow cytometry data

file.name: A02 Well - A02 WLSM.fcs
file.panel: 13-colour B-cell immunophenotyping (CD19, CD3, IgD, IgM, CD27, CD38, CD86, CD80, CD40, HLA-DR, CD197, Zombie-NIR viability)

reasoning: Parsing the FCS file - 19,364 events across 19 channels from a Sony ID7000 spectral cytometer, spectrally unmixed (WLSM).
This is a 13-colour B-cell immunophenotyping panel (CD19, CD3, IgD, IgM, CD27, CD38, CD86, CD80, CD40, HLA-DR, CD197, Zombie-NIR).
arcsinh-transforming the fluorescence channels (cofactor 6000) so positive and negative populations separate cleanly.
Gating sequentially: FSC/SSC to remove debris, then FSC-A vs FSC-H for singlets.
Setting the viability threshold on the Zombie-NIR distribution by Otsu, then isolating live cells.
Identifying the B-cell lineage as CD3-negative, CD19-positive; then characterising subsets and marker expression.
Assembling the figures, the subset table, and a grounded summary - every number traced to the gate that produced it.

items.label: Analysis scope
items.chosen: Exploratory analysis: QC, viability and singlet gating, CD19+ B-cell identification, marker expression summary, and key visualizations (scatter, histograms, heatmap)
alternatives: Manual sequential gating with specific gate hierarchy
Specific subset analysis (memory B cells, plasmablasts)

items.label: Gating strategy
items.chosen: Standard sequential gating: FSC/SSC scatter, singlets (FSC-A vs FSC-H), live cells, CD3-CD19+ B cells, then characterise by IgD, IgM, CD27, CD38, CD86, CD80, CD40, HLA-DR, CD197

items.label: Viability threshold
items.chosen: Data-driven Otsu threshold on the Zombie-NIR distribution

items.label: Visualization approach
items.chosen: FSC/SSC scatter, singlet gate, viability histogram, CD3 vs CD19, IgD/CD27 subsets, marker correlation heatmap

clarifications.question: What type of analysis would you like me to perform on this flow cytometry data?

clarifications.default: Exploratory analysis - QC, gating, marker summary, and key plots

clarifications.question: Which gating approach should I use?
clarifications.context: Standard sequential gating follows: FSC/SSC -> singlets -> live -> CD3-CD19+ B cells -> marker characterisation.

options: Standard sequential manual gating (FSC/SSC -> singlets -> live -> CD3-CD19+)

steps.title: FSC/SSC scatter plot and cell gating

summary: **19,364 total events** acquired on a Sony ID7000 spectral cytometer with a 13-colour B-cell immunophenotyping panel.
**Sequential gating** (FSC/SSC -> singlets -> live -> CD3-CD19+) yielded **6,216 B cells (32.1% of total; 42.1% of live singlets)**, with **85.4% viability**.
**B-cell subsets** (IgD/CD27): **41.1% naive**, 29.4% switched memory, 26.1% double-negative, 3.4% unswitched memory.
**Constitutively expressed markers**: HLA-DR (97.8% positive), CD40 (99.6%), and CD197/CCR7 (63.5%) were high on B cells.
**Activation markers** CD86 (27.4% positive) and CD80 (7.3% positive) showed heterogeneous expression, consistent with a largely resting B-cell population.

figures.caption: Figure 1. FSC-A vs SSC-A scatter of all 19,364 events. Dashed rectangle indicates the cell gate (FSC-A 100k-500k, SSC-A 20k-400k), retaining 92.4% of events.
Figure 2. FSC-A vs FSC-H singlet discrimination. Dashed lines define the singlet gate by FSC-A/FSC-H ratio, retaining 96.5% of gated cells.
Figure 3. Zombie-NIR viability histogram. Otsu threshold separates live (85.4%) from dead events.
Figure 4. CD3 vs CD19 lineage gating on live singlets. CD3-CD19+ B cells: 6,216 events (42.1% of live singlets).
Figure 5. IgD vs CD27 B-cell subsets: naive 41.1%, switched memory 29.4%, double-negative 26.1%, unswitched memory 3.4%.
Figure 6. Spearman rank correlation across 10 B-cell markers.

sections.detailedAnswer: The sample is a viable, predominantly resting B-cell population. After removing debris, doublets, and dead cells, 6,216 CD3-CD19+ B cells were recovered (42.1% of live singlets). The subset structure is naive-dominant (41.1%) with a substantial switched-memory (29.4%) and double-negative (26.1%) compartment and a small unswitched-memory fraction (3.4%). Antigen-presentation machinery (HLA-DR, CD40) is uniformly high, while co-stimulatory activation markers CD86 and CD80 are expressed on a minority of cells, consistent with resting rather than activated B cells.
sections.methods: FCS parsed with fcsparser. Fluorescence channels arcsinh-transformed (cofactor 6000). Per-channel positivity thresholds set by Otsu's method. Cell gate: FSC-A 100k-500k, SSC-A 20k-400k. Singlets: FSC-A/FSC-H ratio between the 3rd and 99.5th percentile. Viability: Zombie-NIR below the Otsu threshold. Lineage: CD3-CD19+. Subsets: IgD/CD27 quadrant gating. Co-expression: Spearman rank correlation. Pipeline is deterministic and reproducible from public/workbook/flow/pipeline.py.
sections.assumptionsNote: Gating is fully automated (Otsu per channel), not FMO-informed; thresholds are data-driven and reproducible. Absolute subset percentages depend on threshold placement.
